_comment_variant: HYDRA Variant B — Brandon Trojan Horse stack with NARROW 5/10pt widths (same as variant C) but a denser 4-slot entry grid (09:45 / 10:45 / 11:15 / 11:45) instead of C's 3 morning slots. 2026-05-13 cut from 7 slots → 4 after 5/4-5/12 review: B went 0-for-4 days at the original 7-slot × 15c sizing, commission drag dominated (each Brandon narrow-spread entry pays $2.50/leg × 4 legs × 15c = $150 round-trip; 7 slots = $1,050/day in fees alone). Dropped the 09:31 opening tick (high whipsaw), the 10:15 between-slot (low marginal info vs neighbors), and the 12:15 late slot (GEX walls already in motion). Same 2026-05-13 change: contracts_per_entry 15 → 10 (same commission-drag logic). Pre-2026-05-05 design used HYDRA wide widths; that experiment was retired because Brandon GEX-ADJ + put-only VIX gate combined to skip B on most days. Dry-run only.

strategy._comment_widths: MKT-027 settings here are unused at runtime — strategy.brandon.narrow_spread.enabled=true overrides _get_vix_adjusted_spread_width with Brandon's 5/10pt rule. Values kept aligned with variant A's VM config (max=75pt) for consistency / debugging clarity.
strategy._comment_put_only_max_vix: Lifted from HYDRA-baseline 15 → 28 because Brandon GEX-ADJ skip-and-route-as-put-only is a core part of his strategy when SPX is inside the call accel zone — capping the fallback at VIX < 15 would block it on most days (VIX 15-25 is normal, not extreme).
strategy._comment_credit_floors: Lowered ~10× vs original variant B because narrow_spread.enabled=true makes the IC 5pt or 10pt wide instead of 75pt — credits scale roughly proportionally with spread width, so HYDRA's standard $0.20/$0.30 floors would skip every entry.
strategy._comment_safety_caps: ORDER-006 caps tuned for B's 4-slot grid at 10c (2026-05-13 cut from 7×15c). Per-order cap 15 leaves headroom; per-underlying cap 60 = 1.5 ICs (10c × 4 legs × 1.5) — keeps B from monopolizing the shared $50,741 Saxo margin pool when A and C are also placing trades. With 6-IC account-wide concurrent-open ceiling, B's slice is ~1-2 simultaneous; the rest of B's 4 slots fire sequentially as TPs cycle.
strategy._comment_min_bp_per_ic: ORDER-004 BP-per-contract floor lowered $5000 → $500 because narrow 5pt × $100 = $500/contract margin (Brandon narrow-spread economics). Default is sized for wide HYDRA-baseline (50pt × $100 = $5000); without this override, every entry trips required vs available — exactly what blocked all 7 entries on 2026-05-06.
strategy._comment_pnl_bounds: DATA-003 sanity bounds, scaled for narrow-spread × 10c (2026-05-13 cut from 15c). PER-CONTRACT figures multiplied by contracts at runtime: max_pnl_per_ic 100 × 10c = $1000 ceiling (covers full credit on 10pt high-VIX worst case), min_pnl_per_ic -1000 × 10c = -$10,000 floor (covers max loss 10pt × $100 × 10c = $10k). Without these overrides the 1c-baseline -$3000 floor would silently skip stop checks once a real drawdown developed.
strategy._comment_downday_callonly: OFF on B (and should be OFF on C) — Brandon GEX adjuster is the sole side-selection logic on the Brandon stack; HYDRA's down-day call-only override would conflict with GEX zone reads.

vix_regime._comment_max_entries: After 2026-05-13 trim to 4 base slots, ceiling stays at 4 in the two lowest VIX zones (let all 4 fire if VIX < 22), then steps down with risk. High-VIX zones still capped because 10pt × 10c = $10k margin per IC.
vix_regime._comment_min_credits: Lowered ~10× vs variant A because narrow_spread.enabled=true makes the IC 5pt or 10pt wide instead of 75pt — credits scale roughly proportionally with spread width.

directional_pivot._comment: Disabled in variant B — Brandon GEX-breach exit (live) plays the same role.

brandon._comment: Same Brandon stack as variant C (narrow 5/10pt widths). All Brandon features LIVE: TP, narrow_spread, GEX strike adjuster, GEX breach exit, defensive overlay. Only shadow behavior is hydra_stop_shadow for head-to-head comparison.
brandon._comment_disable_tightening: MKT-020/MKT-022 progressive tightening is HYDRA's credit-chasing mechanism — it walks strikes inward when initial credit < floor. Brandon's strategy picks strikes per GEX zones and accepts low credit at safe strikes; if credit < gate the entry should SKIP, not move strikes onto a wall. 2026-05-07 incident: tightener walked B's E#5/E#6 puts from 125pt OTM (safe) to 35-40pt OTM (right on the 7330 GEX wall) chasing credit; when SPX broke the wall at 13:26 the resulting 4 breach exits cost ~$8.7K. With this flag set, MKT-020/MKT-022 skip on B and the strike stays where Brandon GEX-ADJ wants it.
brandon._comment_delta_target: Brandon-faithful strike selection: anchor short strikes to a delta target read from Polygon's option chain (8 delta default, inherited from strategy.target_delta). Replaces HYDRA's 'starting OTM = N × expected_move' approximation, which underestimates real risk on low-VIX days. When enabled, _calculate_strikes asks the GEX profile for the strike at the configured delta; downstream HYDRA pipeline (Brandon GEX-ADJ for accel-zone skip, narrow-spread width, MKT-045 chain snap) runs on top unchanged. Falls back to OTM-multiplier if Polygon greeks are unavailable.

hydra_stop_shadow._comment: When true, computes HYDRA's existing credit+buffer stop every tick in parallel without acting. First fire per side per day is Telegrammed for head-to-head comparison against Brandon's GEX breach (which IS the live stop in B/C).

external_price_feed._comment: Yahoo Finance fallback for VIX/SPX when Saxo returns NoAccess. shared/config_loader.py defaults enabled=false in cloud — explicit override here because Saxo's VIX feed flaps NoAccess every 10-15 sec on FullTradingAndChat session contention. Yahoo fallback silences the noise and prevents downstream None-VIX bugs in spread-width / vix_regime calculations. Read-only data so it doesn't compromise the LIVE-trading boundary.

google_sheets._comment: Top-level google_sheets block — schema GoogleSheetsLogger.__init__ actually reads. Mirrors variant A's block, only spreadsheet_name differs.
